title: Compliance helper stopped working: 'permission denied' reading the audit log
description_md:
The `auditor` account (no sudo, deliberately narrow access) reviews `/var/log/secure/access.log` for compliance using a small helper, `/usr/local/bin/read-audit-log`, that's meant to let it read that one root-owned file without granting broader access. Since a 'permissions hardening' pass ran across `/usr/local/bin`, the helper fails for `auditor` with a permission error, even though `ls -l` shows it's still owned by root and still executable. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), work out what the hardening pass actually stripped, and restore the helper to working order.

You're done when the `auditor` user can run `read-audit-log` and it actually prints the log contents.
